<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q18337174]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sonai-jinja 01.jpg | location = 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.456972|N|136.655472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** {{ill|Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja|曽奈比比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mimae-no-kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Shiraki-san (白木さん) | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.

(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>

Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>

'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宮本村 (三重県)]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area05/area05_01.jpg 園相神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0608.html 園相神社（そないじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神道 横}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Rituals ==
During major festivals such as {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary offerings, priests visit the shrine to conduct rituals at the shrine front.<ref name="sk0"/> Other ceremonies, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, are conducted remotely from the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="sk0"/>
Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Flora ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337174]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sonai-jinja 01.jpg | location = 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.456972|N|136.655472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** {{ill|Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja|曽奈比比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mimae-no-kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Shiraki-san (白木さん) | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.

ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宮本村 (三重県)]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area05/area05_01.jpg 園相神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0608.html 園相神社（そないじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神道 横}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Rituals ==
During major festivals such as {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary offerings, priests visit the shrine to conduct rituals at the shrine front.<ref name="sk0"/> Other ceremonies, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, are conducted remotely from the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="sk0"/>
Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Flora ==
[[File:Sonai-jinja 02.jpg|thumb|Sacred forest of Sonai Shrine and Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line]]
}}

However, from around the [[:en:Ōnin|Ōnin era]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337174]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sonai-jinja 01.jpg | location = 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.456972|N|136.655472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** {{ill|Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja|曽奈比比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mimae-no-kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Shiraki-san (白木さん) | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宮本村 (三重県)]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area05/area05_01.jpg 園相神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0608.html 園相神社（そないじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神道 横}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Rituals ==
During major festivals such as {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary offerings, priests visit the shrine to conduct rituals at the shrine front.<ref name="sk0"/> Other ceremonies, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, are conducted remotely from the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="sk0"/>
Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>

'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area05/area05_01.jpg 園相神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0608.html 園相神社（そないじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神道 横}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Rituals ==
During major festivals such as {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary offerings, priests visit the shrine to conduct rituals at the shrine front.<ref name="sk0"/> Other ceremonies, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, are conducted remotely from the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="sk0"/>
Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Flora ==

'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.

{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Rituals ==
During major festivals such as {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary offerings, priests visit the shrine to conduct rituals at the shrine front.<ref name="sk0"/> Other ceremonies, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, are conducted remotely from the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="sk0"/>
Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Flora ==
[[File:Sonai-jinja 02.jpg|thumb|Sacred forest of Sonai Shrine and Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line]]
}}

However, from around the [[:en:Ōnin|Ōnin era]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園相神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337174]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sonai-jinja 01.jpg | location = 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.456972|N|136.655472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** {{ill|Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja|曽奈比比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonaibihiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mimae-no-kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Shiraki-san (白木さん) | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宮本村 (三重県)]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area05/area05_01.jpg 園相神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0608.html 園相神社（そないじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

(1467–1468), reconstruction efforts began to delay, and eventually, not only did reconstruction cease, but the location of the shrine grounds also became unknown.<ref name="sk9"/>
In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (1663), the chief priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga) conducted a search for the old shrine grounds. Referring to descriptions in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō'' such as "located in Numaki-go Sekiryo," the shrine was reconstructed in Sekiryo Village (present-day [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Sekiryo) on [[:en:September 22|September 22]] (Gregorian calendar: October 22, 1663), and the deity was transferred on [[:en:December 9|December 9]] (Gregorian calendar: January 7, 1664).<ref name="sk9"/>
However, doubts arose regarding this location, and on [[:en:December 23|December 23]] of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (Gregorian calendar: February 9, 1692), Ōnakatomi Nagaharu determined that Tsumura was the original site. The shrine was relocated to its current location on [[:en:March 26|March 26]] of Genroku 7 (Gregorian calendar: April 20, 1694).<ref name="sk9"/>
The shrine was rebuilt in March [[:en:1958|1958]] (Shōwa 33), and major renovations were carried out in April [[:en:1981|1981]] (Shōwa 56).<ref name="sk1"/>

Additionally, the role of {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Hafuri (Shinto)|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafuri (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} is designated to carry out daily maintenance and shrine duties. The hafuri are appointed by the Jingū Administration Office.<ref name="sk0"/>

'''{{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Sonai Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks third among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine enshrines deities who serve as the guardians of the sacred gardens (*御園*, *misono*) of the Grand Shrine.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 71.</ref>
== Overview ==
Located at 723 Shiraki, Tsumura-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="tm"/> This shrine is said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]].<ref name="gk"/> Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Shiraki-san" or "Shiroki-no-miya," and has garnered deep devotion.<ref name="ic"/>
Walking through a straight path lined with tall {{ill|cedar trees|ja|杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cedar trees|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スギ|1=Cryptomeria japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q147388}}, one reaches the shrine buildings.<ref name="ic"/> At the entrance of the path stands a stone inscribed in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] (1724), prohibiting the killing of living beings.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 221</ref> As of 1990 (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds covered 5,609 m², but 753.9 m² were transferred to Mie Prefecture for the expansion of {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="sk1"/> The shrine buildings are constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with board roofing, measuring approximately 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7 shaku (≈2.1 m) in width, and 7.8 shaku (≈2.4 m) in height.<ref name="sk1"/> They are equipped with a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]]), a tamagaki gate, and a [[:en:Torii|torii]].<ref name="sk1"/>
In ancient times, there were two shrine buildings, each measuring 9 shaku (≈2.7 m) in length, 7.5 shaku (≈2.3 m) in width, and 4.6 shaku (≈1.4 m) in height.<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref> The sacred object is a stone.<ref name="jg"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
The enshrined deities are Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto (曽奈比比古命) and Gozen-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="tm"/><ref name="ic"/> They are local deities of this region<ref name="gk"/> and protectors of the sacred gardens of Ise Grand Shrine.<ref name="ic"/> Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto is the child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命) and is known as the deity of garden creation.<ref name="tm"/>
There was once a belief that the shrine could cure toothaches, and it was customary to offer 12 pairs of chopsticks made from bush clover as a token of gratitude upon recovery.<ref name="ic"/>
In ancient times, there was an independent shrine dedicated to Gozen-no-Kami, but after its reconstruction in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 3 (1266), no further reconstructions took place. Eventually, the shrine was not rebuilt, and Gozen-no-Kami came to be enshrined together with Sonaibihiko-no-Mikoto in the main shrine.<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 219–220</ref>
== History ==
It is said that when [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] traveled along the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]], she dedicated sacred gardens to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] at this location, thus establishing the shrine.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 220</ref> Shrines enshrining {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} were traditionally rebuilt using public funds, but six highly Ranked shrines, including this one, were rebuilt by the shrine’s construction officials after the Completion tier 4]] The shrine forest is composed of large trees that survived the [[:en:Typhoon Vera|Isewan Typhoon]], including [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Japanese cypress]], [[:en:Cryptomeria|cedar]], and [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Japanese black pine]]. It also contains a mixture of evergreen broadleaf trees such as [[:en:Cleyera japonica|sakaki]], [[:en:Castanopsis|Japanese chinquapin]], {{ill|black oak|ja|クロバイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/black oak|1=Symplocos prunifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5793139}}, and [[:en:Eurya japonica|Japanese cleyera]], along with deciduous broadleaf trees.<ref name="sk0"/>
== Surroundings ==
The shrine entrance faces {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|ja|三重県道22号伊勢南島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 22 Ise-Nantō Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 22|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357908}}.<ref name="gk"/><ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 70</ref> Despite its location along the roadway, the precincts remain peaceful and serene.<ref name="ic"/> Visitors coming from {{ill|Yamada (Ise)|ja|山田 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada (Ise)|1=Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470062}} pass under the overpass of the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] to reach the shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus and get off at the ''Shiraki'' bus stop. The shrine is located immediately nearby.<ref name="tm"/> (Approx. 7.5 km bus distance,<ref name="sk9"/> about 19 minutes.<ref name="tm"/>)
* By car, it takes about 11 minutes (approx. 7.5 km) from {{ill|Tamaki IC|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki IC|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the Ise Expressway, or about 27 minutes (approx. 7.3 km) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}}.
